title: On The Queenï؟½s Visit To London. The Night Of The Seventeenth Of March 1789.
content:
آ آ آ آ When, long sequesterï؟½d from his throne,
آ آ آ آ George took his seat again,
آ آ آ آ By right of worth, not blood alone,
آ آ آ آ Entitled here to reign,


آ آ آ آ Then loyalty, with all his lamps
آ آ آ آ New trimmï؟½d, a gallant show!
آ آ آ آ Chasing the darkness and the damps,
آ آ آ آ Set London in a glow.


آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twas hard to tell, of streets or squares
آ آ آ آ Which formï؟½d the chief display,
آ آ آ آ These most resembling clusterï؟½d stars,
آ آ آ آ Those the long milky way.


آ آ آ آ Bright shone the roofs, the domes, the spires,
آ آ آ آ And rockets flew, self-driven,
آ آ آ آ To hang their momentary fires
آ آ آ آ Amid the vault of heaven.


آ آ آ آ So, fire with water to compare,
آ آ آ آ The ocean serves, on high
آ آ آ آ Up-spouted by a whale in air,
آ آ آ آ To express unwieldy joy.


آ آ آ آ Had all the pageants of the world
آ آ آ آ In one procession joinï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And all the banners been unfurlï؟½d
آ آ آ آ That heralds eï؟½er designï؟½d,


آ آ آ آ For no such sight had Englandï؟½s queen
آ آ آ آ Forsaken her retreat,
آ آ آ آ Where George, recoverï؟½d, made a scene
آ آ آ آ Sweet always, doubly sweet.


آ آ آ آ Yet glad she came that night to prove,
آ آ آ آ A witness undescried,
آ آ آ آ How much the object of her love
آ آ آ آ Was loved by all beside.


آ آ آ آ Darkness the skies had mantled oï؟½er
آ آ آ آ In aid of her designï؟½
آ آ آ آ Darkness, O Queen! neï؟½er called before
آ آ آ آ To veil a deed of thine!


آ آ آ آ On borrowï؟½d wheels away she flies,
آ آ آ آ Resolved to be unknown,
آ آ آ آ And gratify no curious eyes
آ آ آ آ That night except her own.


آ آ آ آ Arrived, a night like noon she sees,
آ آ آ آ And hears the million hum;
آ آ آ آ As all by instinct, like the bees,
آ آ آ آ Had known their sovereign come.


آ آ آ آ Pleased she beheld, aloft portrayï؟½d
آ آ آ آ On many a splendid wall,
آ آ آ آ Emblems of health and heavenly aid,
آ آ آ آ And George the theme of all.


آ آ آ آ Unlike the enigmatic line,
آ آ آ آ So difficult to spell,
آ آ آ آ Which shook Belshazzar at his wine
آ آ آ آ The night his city fell.


آ آ آ آ Soon watery grew her eyes and dim,
آ آ آ آ But with a joyful tear,
آ آ آ آ None else, except in prayer for him,
آ آ آ آ George ever drew from her.


آ آ آ آ It was a scene in every part
آ آ آ آ Like those in fable feignï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And seemï؟½d by some magicianï؟½s art
آ آ آ آ Created and sustainï؟½d.


آ آ آ آ But other magic there, she knew,
آ آ آ آ Had been exerted none,
آ آ آ آ To raise such wonders in her view,
آ آ آ آ Save love of George alone.


آ آ آ آ That cordial thought her spirit cheerï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And, through the cumbrous throng,
آ آ آ آ Not else unworthy to be fearï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ Conveyï؟½d her calm along.


آ آ آ آ So, ancient poets say, serene
آ آ آ آ The sea-maid rides the waves,
آ آ آ آ And fearless of the billowy scene,
آ آ آ آ Her peaceful bosom laves.


آ آ آ آ With more than astronomic eyes
آ آ آ آ She viewï؟½d the sparkling show;
آ آ آ آ One Georgian star adorns the skies,
آ آ آ آ She myriads found below.


آ آ آ آ Yet let the glories of a night
آ آ آ آ Like that, once seen, suffice,
آ آ آ آ Heaven grant us no such future sight,
آ آ آ آ Such previous woe the price!
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On The Queenï؟½s Visit To London. The Night Of The Seventeenth Of March 1789."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
